---
title: "Runtime comparison of GPD fitting methods"
date: "Compiled at `r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', tz = 'UTC')` UTC"
output:
  github_document: default
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
always_allow_html: true
params:
  name: "02_runtime" # change if you rename file
---

```{r here, message=FALSE}
here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".Rmd"), uuid = "83a2adbb-43b0-41b0-98cc-eeddbe1cedd9")
```

In this script we compare the runtime of the different methods for GPD parameter fitting.

```{r packages}
library(conflicted)
library(microbenchmark)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(eva)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(kableExtra)
```

```{r directories}
# create or *empty* the target directory, used to write this file's data: 
#projthis::proj_create_dir_target(params$name, clean = TRUE)

# function to get path to target directory: path_target("sample.csv")
path_target <- projthis::proj_path_target(params$name)

# function to get path to previous data: path_source("00-import", "sample.csv")
path_source <- projthis::proj_path_source(params$name)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  dev = c("png", "cairo_pdf"),
  fig.ext = c("png", "pdf"),
  fig.width = 10, 
  fig.height = 6, 
  dpi = 200,
  fig.path = paste0('figures/', params, "/gpd_runtime_"),
  message = FALSE, 
  warning = FALSE,
  echo = FALSE)
```

## Load permApprox functions

```{r permapprox-load}
# Load permApprox functions
proj_path <- rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::has_file("README.md"))
permApprox_path <- file.path(proj_path, "functions/permApprox_eps_fct/R")
permApprox_functions <- list.files(permApprox_path, pattern = "\\.R$", full.names = TRUE)
invisible(sapply(permApprox_functions, source))
```

## Set controls

```{r, echo=TRUE}
set.seed(123456)

# Data-generating params (fixed across scenarios)
n_vec         <- c(200, 1000)
shape_true    <- -0.25
scale_true    <- 1

# Boundary settings to *evaluate*:
#   NA  -> unconstrained (boundary = NULL in the fit calls)
#   5   -> constrained (boundary = 5 in the fit calls)
boundary_vec  <- c(NA_real_, 5)

times_per_scn <- 50
unit_choice   <- "ms"
```

```{r}
# Create scenarios: generate x ONCE per n; reuse across boundary settings
scenarios <- tibble::tibble(
  n = n_vec,
  shape = shape_true,
  scale = scale_true,
  x = purrr::map(n, ~ eva::rgpd(n = .x, scale = scale_true, shape = shape_true))
) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(boundary = boundary_vec) %>%
  mutate(boundary_label = ifelse(is.na(boundary), "unconstrained", paste0("boundary=", boundary)))

```

## Helpers

```{r}
# Helper: call each estimator with boundary & eval_point
call_fit <- function(fun, x, boundary = NA_real_) {
  if (is.na(boundary)) {
    fun(x = x, boundary = NULL, eval_point = NULL)
  } else {
    fun(x = x, boundary = boundary, eval_point = boundary)
  }
}

estimators <- list(
  LME    = function(x, boundary) call_fit(.fit_gpd_lme,    x, boundary),
  MLE1D  = function(x, boundary) call_fit(.fit_gpd_mle1d,  x, boundary),
  MLE2D  = function(x, boundary) call_fit(.fit_gpd_mle2d,  x, boundary),
  NLS2   = function(x, boundary) call_fit(.fit_gpd_nls2,   x, boundary),
  WNLLSM = function(x, boundary) call_fit(.fit_gpd_wnllsm, x, boundary),
  ZSE    = function(x, boundary) call_fit(.fit_gpd_zse,    x, boundary)
)

bench_one <- function(x, boundary, times = times_per_scn, 
                      unit = unit_choice) {
  microbenchmark::microbenchmark(
    LME    = estimators$LME(x, boundary),
    MLE1D  = estimators$MLE1D(x, boundary),
    MLE2D  = estimators$MLE2D(x, boundary),
    NLS2   = estimators$NLS2(x, boundary),
    WNLLSM = estimators$WNLLSM(x, boundary),
    ZSE    = estimators$ZSE(x, boundary),
    times = times, unit = unit
  )
}

```

## Run benchmark

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Run benchmark per (n, boundary) using the SAME x per n
results <- scenarios %>%
  mutate(bench = purrr::map2(x, boundary, bench_one)) %>%
  select(n, boundary, boundary_label, bench)

saveRDS(results, file = path_target("bench_results.rds"))
```

## Summarize results

```{r}
# Summarize results and sort by runtime (fastest first)
results <- readRDS(path_target("bench_results.rds"))

summary_tbl <- results %>%
  mutate(smmry = purrr::map(bench, ~ tibble::as_tibble(summary(.x)))) %>%
  select(-bench) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(smmry) %>%
  dplyr::rename(method = expr) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method = gsub("^\\s+|\\s+$", "", method)) %>%
  dplyr::select(n, boundary_label, boundary, method, min, lq, median, uq, max, mean) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(n, boundary_label, median)

summary_tbl %>% kableExtra::kable()
```


```{r}
# Create the grouped table
median_wide <- summary_tbl %>%
  group_by(n, boundary_label) %>%
  arrange(median, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(rank = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(n, boundary_label, rank, method, median)

# Make a nice title
cat("\nMedian runtimes (", unit_choice, ") by scenario (fastest first):\n", sep = "")

# Add a separator for each scenario
median_wide %>%
  arrange(n, boundary_label, rank) %>%
  kableExtra::kable(col.names = c("n", "Boundary", "Rank", "Method", "Median (ms)")) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::group_rows(
    index = table(with(median_wide, paste0("n = ", n, ", ", boundary_label)))
  )

```

```{r runtime_gpd_methods, fig.width=10, fig.height=7}
my_cols <- c(
  "LME"    = "#009E73", 
  "MLE1D"  = "#56B4E9", 
  "MLE2D"  = "#0072B2",
  "NLS2"   = "#E69F00",
  "WNLLSM" = "#D55E00",
  "ZSE"    = "#CC79A7"
)

# Prepare long-format data for ggplot
plot_data <- results %>%
  dplyr::mutate(tib = purrr::map(bench, ~ tibble::as_tibble(.x))) %>%
  dplyr::select(n, boundary_label, tib) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(tib) %>%
  dplyr::rename(method = expr, runtime_ns = time) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    method = trimws(method),
    runtime_ms = runtime_ns / 1e6
  )

# Order methods by median runtime (so y-axis consistent)
plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(method) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method = forcats::fct_reorder(method, runtime_ms, median)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# Ensure boundary labels are ordered manually (left = unconstrained)
plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    boundary_label = factor(boundary_label,
                            levels = c("unconstrained", "boundary=5"))
  )

# Make LME bold
method_labels <- setNames(
  lapply(levels(factor(plot_data$method)), function(m) {
    if (m == "LME") bquote(bold(.(m) * "*")) else m
  }),
  levels(factor(plot_data$method))
)

# Boxplots ŌĆö facet by n and boundary_label
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = method, y = runtime_ms, fill = method)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = 21, outlier.size = 1.5, width = 0.65) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_cols) +
  facet_wrap(~ n + boundary_label, 
             labeller = labeller(boundary_label = label_value, 
                                 n = label_both)) +
  labs(
    title = "Runtime distribution of GPD fitting methods",
    subtitle = paste0("Each box shows ", times_per_scn, 
                      " runs per method ├Ś boundary setting"),
    x = "Method", y = "Runtime (ms)"
  ) +
  scale_y_log10(
    breaks = c(1, 3, 10, 30, 100, 1000),
    labels = c("1", "3", "10", "30", "100", "1000")
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = method_labels) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```

* NLS2 and WNLLSM are the slowest methods across all settings.
* The runtime of ZSE is highly dependent on the setting (fastest for small n with constraint)
* MLE2D is the fastest for large n but does also perform well for small n
* The performance of MLE1D and LME depends on the constraint:
  + In the unconstrained cases LME is faster
  + In the constrained case MLE1D is faster

## Files written

These files have been written to the target directory, ```r paste0("data/", params$name)```:

```{r list-files-target}
projthis::proj_dir_info(path_target())
```
